hypothesis: F-PSY4: personality archetypes predict research methodology effectiveness. H1: deep-investigation types (experimenter) produce 3x more paradigm shifts. H2: precision-focused types (tooler) achieve 2x higher execution precision. H3: collaborative types (historian) produce 50% more cross-domain findings.
method: Extracted role, mode, status, artifact presence, paradigm-shift indicators, cross-domain references, and prediction-match rates from 77 DOMEX lanes in SWARM-LANES.md. 51 lanes with explicit role= tags (25 experimenter, 10 historian, 16 tooler). Paradigm shift proxy: FALSIFIED/DOWNGRADE/PHIL mentions in actual+diff+notes fields. Precision proxy: merge_rate + prediction_match. Cross-domain proxy: reference to domains other than lane's own. Effect sizes computed as Cohen's d.
expect: H1 CONFIRMED (experimenter 3x paradigm shift rate), H2 CONFIRMED (tooler 2x precision), H3 CONFIRMED (historian 50% more cross-domain). Role-mode alignment improves merge rate by 10%+.
actual: H1 PARTIALLY CONFIRMED: experimenter 52% paradigm-shift rate vs 15% baseline (3.5x, d=+0.826 large). But mechanism is role-general, NOT falsification-specific (33% falsification vs 55% other modes). H2 PARTIALLY CONFIRMED: tooler merge=100% prediction_match=87.5% (highest precision), but experimenter nearly matches (96%/92%). Effect: toolers AVOID paradigm shifts (d=-0.661). H3 FALSIFIED: historian 0% cross-domain (vs experimenter 68%). Historian HHI=1.0 (all 10 lanes in META domain). Role-mode alignment HURTS: aligned pairs merge 75% vs misaligned 97.4%.
diff: H1 direction correct but mechanism wrong: not introversion/falsification, just the experimenter identity. H2 confirmed for completion precision, but experimenters rival on prediction accuracy. H3 completely reversed — experimenters are the cross-domain producers, historians are tunnel-visioned. Biggest surprise: role-mode alignment is ANTI-correlated with success (75% vs 97.4%). This contradicts the personality-methodology 'fit' model.
key_findings: Experimenter role IS the paradigm-shift engine (52%, d=+0.826). Not methodology-specific — the identity drives the behavior.
Tooler role IS the reliability engine (100% merge, 0% abandon). Trades paradigm shifts for execution certainty.
Historian role has structural tunnel vision: HHI=1.0 (only META domain). Zero cross-domain findings despite being the 'synthesizer' archetype.
Role-mode alignment is ANTI-productive: misaligned pairs outperform aligned (97.4% vs 75% merge). Productive tension > comfortable fit.
Unassigned lanes (early DOMEX era) have 26.9% artifact rate vs 100% for all assigned roles — role assignment itself is the primary intervention.
prescription: dispatch_optimizer.py should assign experimenter for paradigm-shift frontiers, tooler for hardening/infrastructure, and redistribute historian across domains (not just META)
Consider ANTI-matching role-mode: assign experimenters to hardening, toolers to exploration — productive friction hypothesis
Role assignment alone (vs unassigned) is the biggest effect: 100% artifact rate vs 26.9%. Tag ALL lanes.
cross_domain: Connects to expert-swarm (F-SWARMER2: colony role optimization), evaluation (F-EVAL2: how to measure expert effectiveness), and organizational psychology (role-task fit literature shows 'misfit' can increase creativity — Miron-Spektor & Beenen 2015)
